title: Some States Opt Out of Trump’s ‘Great American State Fair’
description: At least seven have declined to participate, the latest sign that the national 250th birthday celebration has become a fragmented and partisan affair as the president seeks his imprint.
author: Eileen Sullivan

title: SpaceX’s I.P.O. Will Help Fulfill Elon Musk’s Political Vision of America
description: The SpaceX I.P.O. will enable its founder to build — and scale — his peculiar vision of society.
author: Amy Gamerman and Tam Stockton

title: The World Is Draining Oil Reserves, Raising Pressure for a Peace Deal
description: The amount of oil and fuel stored by businesses and governments has fallen sharply since the start of the U.S.-Israeli war against Iran.
author: Rebecca F. Elliott and Lazaro Gamio

title: This Could Be the Winning Issue for Democrats
description: A.I. is not inevitable.
author: Tressie McMillan Cottom

title: ‘All Men Are Created Equal’? Not Everyone Agrees.
description: Our founding creed has always had its critics.
author: Kim Phillips-Fein

title: Social Security Is Going Broke. Where Is the Outrage?
description: We’ve been here before, but never so close.
author: Jason Furman

title: An Upscale Hotel Breaks Ground in Downtown Newburgh — the First in 133 Years
description: A boutique hotel group known for pioneering tourism in the pastoral Western Catskills has set its sights on one of the Hudson Valley’s most complicated downtowns.
author: Alexandra Marvar and Kate Warren

title: Some Leftists Are Boycotting This Film. Everyone Should Watch It.
description: The inanity of the leftists who’d censor such a film shouldn’t distract us from the right-wing nightmare it reveals.
author: Michelle Goldberg

title: Nobody Drinks Bordeaux in Bordeaux
description: In restaurants and wine bars in Bordeaux, plenty of other wines are open, but few bottles of the local product. Why?
author: Eric Asimov

title: Scientists Measure Earth’s Vast Underground Fungal Webs
description: With machine learning and a high-resolution imaging robot, scientists measured and mapped the extent of Earth’s carbon circulatory system.
author: Emily Anthes

title: Joe Negri, Handyman and Music Maestro on ‘Mister Rogers,’ Dies at 99
description: An accomplished jazz guitarist, he appeared on more than 300 episodes of the public television show, playing alongside musicians like Wynton Marsalis and Yo-Yo Ma.
author: Michael S. Rosenwald

title: Kennedy Center Appeals Order to Remove Trump’s Name
description: One day before a deadline to take the president’s name off its facade, the arts institution appealed a federal judge’s ruling that also temporarily blocked it from closing.
author: Julia Jacobs

title: Inside Cesar Chavez’s Labor Union, Women Faced a Culture of Sexual Abuse
description: Many of the women who helped lead the fight for Latino civil rights say they were quietly waging their own battles with harassment, misogyny and sexual assault inside the powerful United Farm Workers union.
author: Sarah Hurtes and Manny Fernandez

title: U.S. Plan Is Said to Pull a Third of Fighter Jets It Provides NATO for Europe
description: The plan, outlined by officials and in a written document, provides rare clarity about the extent to which the Trump administration intends to reduce its commitment to NATO.
author: Christopher F. Schuetze and Eric Schmitt

title: Trump Suspends Funding for Los Angeles Homeless Agency
description: The Trump administration cited misspending among the reasons for blocking funds to the Los Angeles Homeless Services Authority. Mayor Karen Bass warned that “people will lose their lives.”
author: Jill Cowan

title: House Rejects Bill to Extend Surveillance Power With FISA Section 702 Set to Expire
description: A measure to temporarily continue a key surveillance law failed in the House, and members left for a weeklong recess, making an expiration all but inevitable.
author: Robert Jimison and Dustin Volz

title: Postal Service Issues Proposal to Block Mail Ballots in States That Don’t Turn Over Data
description: Democrats and voting-rights groups have challenged the proposed rule as a harmful federal intrusion that could affect millions of voters who cast their ballots by mail.
author: Adam Sella and Nick Corasaniti

title: Justice Dept. Pushes Limits of Its Power Over State Elections
description: The department once tried to stay out of state elections, urging caution. It is now pressing forward with claims of fraud as President Trump revives his unfounded assertions that elections cannot be trusted.
author: Devlin Barrett

title: Trump and Hegseth Broadcast U.S. Military Strikes Before They Happen
description: Commanders do not usually speak publicly about future operations to avoid jeopardizing the mission’s success.
author: Eric Schmitt

title: After Senate Loss, Cornyn Predicts ‘Miserable’ Final Two Years for Trump
description: In his first extensive interview since his defeat by a Trump-backed challenger, the Texas Republican said the Senate was in for a “bumpy ride” as he and others flex new political freedom.
author: Carl Hulse
